SEARCH
Package minum.logging

Class LoggingActionQueue

java.lang.Object
minum.logging.LoggingActionQueue
public class LoggingActionQueue extends Object
This class is very similar to ActionQueue but is focused on Logging.

The only reason it was necessary to create a wholly separate stack for logging is that logging has so many circular dependencies with the rest of the system, this was the only way to free ourselves during initialization / shutdown.

    void
    enqueue(String description, ThrowingRunnable<?> action)
    Adds something to the queue to be processed.
    LoggingActionQueue
    initialize()
     
    void
    stop()
    This will prevent any new actions being queued (by setting the stop flag to true and thus causing an exception to be thrown when a call is made to [enqueue]) and will block until the queue is empty.
    void
    stop(int count, int sleepTime)
    Stops the action queue

  • Constructor Details

    • LoggingActionQueue

      public LoggingActionQueue(String name, ExecutorService executorService, Constants constants)

  • Method Details

    • initialize

      public LoggingActionQueue initialize()

    • enqueue

      public void enqueue(String description, ThrowingRunnable<?> action)
      Adds something to the queue to be processed.
      Parameters:
      action - an action to take with no return value. (this uses callable so we can collect exceptions)

    • stop

      public void stop(int count, int sleepTime)
      Stops the action queue
      Parameters:
      count - how many loops to wait before we crash it closed
      sleepTime - how long to wait in milliseconds between loops

    • stop

      public void stop()
      This will prevent any new actions being queued (by setting the stop flag to true and thus causing an exception to be thrown when a call is made to [enqueue]) and will block until the queue is empty.

    • toString

      public String toString()
      Overrides:
      toString in class Object
